title: Highway 10 (Saudi Arabia) - Wikipedia
content:
### Highway 10 Straight Section Details

| Feature | Specification |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Start Point** | Haradh |
| **End Point** | Al Batha |
| **Length** | 240 kilometers (150 mi) |
| **Characteristics** | No bends; no noticeable gradients |
| **Geographic Location** | Rub' al Khali desert (Empty Quarter) |
| **World Record Status** | World's longest straight road |
| **Historical Precedent** | Surpassed the 146-kilometer (91 mi) straight section of Australia's Eyre Highway |
| **Original Purpose** | Private road for King Fahd of Saudi Arabia |

### Highway 10 General Information

| Property | Data Point |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Total Route Length** | 1,480 kilometers (920 mi) |
| **Route Start Point** | Ad Darb, Al Darb Governorate |
| **Route End Point** | Saudi–UAE border near the Persian Gulf |
| **Country** | Saudi Arabia |
| **Provinces Traversed** | Jazan, 'Asir, Riyadh, Eastern |
| **Kilometer Marker Mentioned** | 1472 (referenced in 2022) |

### Information Not Stated in the Page
*   **Start and end coordinates:** Specific latitude and longitude for the straight section are not provided.
*   **Mile markers:** Specific mile markers for the start and end of the 240 km section are not provided.
*   **Intersecting highways:** The page does not list highways that intersect with Highway 10.
